name: KrillApp.Trigger
title: Trigger
description: A container node that visually organizes trigger nodes under a DataPoint. Grouping is visual organization only — each trigger observes the DataPoint through its own meta.sources (and reads it via meta.inputs), not through the container. Specific trigger behavior is implemented by nodes such as HighThreshold, LowThreshold, CronTimer, Button, Color, or IncomingWebHook.
shortDescription: Container that visually groups trigger nodes; each trigger wires its own sources to the DataPoint.
llmPurpose: Visually organizes trigger nodes under a DataPoint. Has no execution semantics of its own — each trigger wires its own sources and inputs to the DataPoint it watches.
llmBehavior: contains trigger child nodes for visual organization
no execution semantics — each trigger observes the DataPoint via its own sources and invocationTriggers
llmOutputs: none — child triggers fire via their own wiring
llmTypicalUseCases: group multiple triggers under a single DataPoint
llmPromptHints: add triggers to this data point
set up alerts on this value
llmCreationHints: Create a Trigger node as a child of a DataPoint when the user wants to organize conditional or scheduled logic for that data stream. Remember the grouping is visual only — each trigger must list the DataPoint in its own sources (and inputs) to watch it.

llmConnectionHints.role: organizational container between a DataPoint and specific trigger implementations; wiring lives on each trigger
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Not wired — visual grouping only; each child trigger lists the DataPoint in its own sources (SOURCE_INVOKED) and inputs.

llmExamples: Add a trigger to this temperature sensor.
I want to be alerted when this value changes.
Set up monitoring on this data point.
